subject: [PATCH v6 0/2] mm: improve write performance with RWF_DONTCACHE

reviews.author: Jeff Layton (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged that there were additional issues to address, confirmed they are working on fixes and will post a new version (v7) early next week.
reviews.raw_body:
I saw those after I sent the last set and have been working on
addressing them. I've also found a couple more via dueling Gemini and
Claude reviews.

I'll have a v7 posting coming early next week.

Thanks,
-- 
Jeff Layton <jlayton@kernel.org>

2026-05-09.patch_summary: This patch series improves write performance when using the RWF_DONTCACHE flag by addressing several bugs and optimizing the handling of dirty pages. The changes include tracking DONTCACHE dirty pages per bdi_writeback, kicking the flusher thread instead of initiating writeback inline, and adding a mechanism to run 'perf lock' around testcases. Additionally, the patch series includes several performance optimizations, such as using atomic operations and moving function calls outside locked sections to prevent lost wakeups and spin_unlock_irq() re-enabling interrupts while holding the i_pages xa_lock.
